title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
plain_summary: This bill makes a permanent rule that the federal government cannot use taxpayer money to pay for abortions or health insurance plans that cover abortions. The only times this rule does not apply is when a woman becomes pregnant from rape or incest, or when her life is in danger and she needs an abortion to save her life. The bill also says that federal buildings and federal workers cannot provide abortions. Additionally, health insurance companies must clearly tell people if their plans cover abortions and how much extra that coverage costs.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of twenty twenty five. The law has two main parts. The first part stops the government from using tax money to pay for abortions. The second part explains how this rule applies to health insurance under the Affordable Care Act.

sections.title: Section 101 Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions
sections.text: This section creates a new chapter in federal law that stops the government from using tax money to pay for abortions. The new chapter has nine main rules. First, no federal money can be used to pay for any abortion. Second, no federal money can be used to pay for health insurance that covers abortions. Third, federal hospitals and federal doctors cannot perform abortions as part of their government jobs. Fourth, people can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own money as long as no federal money is used. Fifth, private insurance companies can still offer abortion coverage as long as no federal money pays for it. Sixth, this new law does not change other existing federal laws about abortion funding. Seventh, doctors can still treat medical problems that happen after an abortion. Eighth, federal money can be used for abortions in cases of rape or incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. Ninth, these rules also apply to Washington D.C.

sections.title: Section 102 Amendment to Table of Chapters
sections.text: This section adds the new chapter about stopping taxpayer funded abortions to the official table of contents in federal law.

sections.title: Section 201 Clarifying Application of Prohibition to Premium Credits and Cost-sharing Reductions Under ACA
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to stop people from getting government help to pay for health insurance that covers abortions. People cannot use premium tax credits (government money that helps pay monthly insurance costs) or cost-sharing reductions (government help that lowers out-of-pocket costs) for health plans that cover abortions. However, people can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own money. Insurance companies can still offer abortion coverage, but the government will not help pay for it. Small businesses also cannot get tax credits for buying their employees health insurance that covers abortions. Multi-state health plans sold through government marketplaces cannot cover abortions either. These changes start for tax years ending after December thirty first, twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 202 Revision of Notice Requirements Regarding Disclosure of Extent of Health Plan Coverage of Abortion and Abortion Premium Surcharges
sections.text: This section requires health insurance plans to clearly tell people whether they cover abortions. Insurance companies must put this information in enrollment materials, advertising, comparison tools, and benefit summaries. The information must be easy to see and understand. If a health plan covers abortions and charges extra money for that coverage, the extra charge must be listed separately and clearly labeled. These disclosure rules start thirty days after this law is passed.
